content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings SSS and RRR. Each of these strings has length NNN. We want to make SSS equal to RRR by performing the following operation some number of times (possibly zero):


Choose two integers aaa and bbb such that 1‚â§a‚â§b‚â§N1 \le a \le b \le N1‚â§a‚â§b‚â§N.
For each iii such that a‚â§i‚â§ba \le i \le ba‚â§i‚â§b, replace the iii-th character of SSS by the iii-th character of RRR.
Suppose that we make SSS equal to RRR by performing this operation kkk times, in such a way that the total number of replaced characters (i.e. the sum of all kkk values of b‚àía+1b-a+1b‚àía+1) is lll. Then, the cost of this process is defined as k‚ãÖlk \cdot lk‚ãÖl.

Find the minimum cost with which we can make SSS equal to RRR.
constraints: SSS and RRR contain only lowercase English letters
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1062 \cdot 10^62‚ãÖ106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
The second line contains a single string RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the minimum cost.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: SSS can be made equal to RRR in two moves. First, we replace S1S_1S1‚Äã by R1R_1R1‚Äã and then replace S5S_5S5‚Äã by R5R_5R5‚Äã. We have k=l=2k = l = 2k=l=2, so the cost is 2‚ãÖ2=42 \cdot 2 = 42‚ãÖ2=4. If we wanted to perform only one operation, the cost would be 555.
